methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $266M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Mythic is a Series D chip design company that has raised $266M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (SambaNova Systems at $5.0B, Tenstorrent at $2.7B, PsiQuantum at $7.0B, and DEEPX at $529M) alongside reference to 3 public semiconductor peers (NVIDIA, AMD, Analog Devices).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-Progression, using the $266M raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yields $399M. Peer-Median triangulation across the 4 comparable private companies produces a $3.9B implied valuation, with the peer range spanning $529M to $7.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 24.4x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied without disclosure of Mythic's revenue.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $339M (low) to $4.4B (high), with a median of $2.1B. This wide spread reflects material stage and business-model dispersion within the peer set. The median is heavily influenced by the private-peer cluster, which dominates the signal. Key uncertainties include Mythic's revenue run-rate relative to peers, customer concentration, and go-to-market progress—none of which are specified in available data.
